question.text: Commodity indices in India are constructed from the prices of:

answer.text: Nearby futures contracts of the commodity constituents
Spot prices of the underlying physical commodities
Average of spot and futures prices of commodities

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Commodity indices in India are constructed from the prices of the nearby futures contracts of the commodity constituents, instead of the underlying spot prices. **The Trap:** Unlike equity indices which use current market prices of stocks, commodity indices use futures prices. Spot prices, forward prices, or averages are not the basis. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) covers equity index construction from spot/cash market prices — a key difference from commodity indices. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Since commodity indices use futures prices, they inherently include carrying cost elements — making them total return indices that capture both price changes and roll-over income.
